level1_segments.summary: The assistant surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and manipulates it with a bone saw and other instruments.
The assistant surgeon prepares and cleans the patient while holding surgical instruments.
The assistant surgeon assists the head surgeon by touching the patient.
The assistant surgeon uses a hammer and surgical instruments to hammer the patient.

The assistant surgeon performs a continuous sequence of tasks involving holding instruments, touching the patient, manipulating the robotic arm, assisting the head surgeon, and hammering, interspersed with brief pauses.
The assistant surgeon prepares the surgical site and instruments, including brief hammering and handling various tools.
The assistant surgeon uses a drill on the patient and then prepares the instrument table.
The assistant surgeon performs suturing on the patient and continues to handle instruments and touch the patient.
The assistant surgeon holds instruments and briefly touches the patient while preparing the instrument table with short pauses in between.
The assistant surgeon prepares the patient and manages surgical drapes, interspersed with brief moments of holding instruments or pausing.

level2_segments.summary: The assistant surgeon calibrates and manipulates the Mako robotic arm to prepare for the procedure.
The assistant surgeon supports the main surgical workflow by preparing the site, assisting with bone work, and managing instruments and robotic tools.
The assistant surgeon performs wound closure through suturing and manages final site preparation and draping.

level1_segments.summary: The scrub nurse hands the hammer to the assistant surgeon, who uses it for two brief hammering intervals.
The hammer remains idle with the scrub nurse for an extended period of over 11 minutes.

level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient with a brief pause in between.
The head surgeon drills the patient and handles surgical instruments with short pauses.
The head surgeon continuously touches the patient and holds surgical instruments with intermittent brief pauses.
The head surgeon performs final touches and instrument handling on the patient with minor interruptions.
The head surgeon prepares the surgical site by holding instruments and touching the patient with brief pauses.
The head surgeon calibrates and manipulates the Mako robotic arm while holding a bone saw, interspersed with short pauses.
The head surgeon performs an extended bone sawing procedure using the Mako robotic arm with brief interruptions.
The head surgeon briefly handles surgical instruments and touches the patient between pauses.
The head surgeon continues sawing the patient with the Mako robotic arm before a short pause.
The head surgeon touches the patient and holds surgical instruments with intermittent pauses.
The head surgeon hammers the patient and then resumes touching the patient with instruments, separated by brief pauses.
The head surgeon performs continuous manipulation of the patient's tissue using surgical instruments with brief pauses.
The head surgeon applies cement and hammers the implant into place, followed by final adjustments and a concluding pause.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient and performs initial manual bone work and site preparation.
The head surgeon utilizes the Mako robotic arm to perform precise bone sawing and tissue manipulation.
The head surgeon completes the procedure by hammering the implant, applying cement, and making final adjustments.

level1_segments.summary: The surgical instrument is held by the scrub nurse during an initial idle period.
The robot technician holds the instrument, followed by a brief shared hold with the scrub nurse.
The scrub nurse holds the instrument for an extended period while it remains idle.
The robot technician and scrub nurse briefly share the instrument before the scrub nurse holds it alone again.
The assistant surgeon and scrub nurse exchange the instrument multiple times in a series of short, alternating holds.
The head surgeon and scrub nurse interact with the instrument through alternating and shared holding periods.
The head surgeon, assistant surgeon, and scrub nurse engage in a complex, rapid sequence of shared and individual holds involving all three team members.
The surgical instrument is actively used by the head surgeon with brief transfers to the scrub nurse and assistant surgeon before being held by the scrub nurse for an extended period.
The instrument is rapidly passed between the head surgeon, assistant surgeon, and scrub nurse during a brief collaborative maneuver.
Following a long idle period, the instrument undergoes a series of quick exchanges among the head surgeon, assistant surgeon, and scrub nurse before being held by the scrub nurse.
The surgical instrument is repeatedly transferred between the head surgeon and the scrub nurse during a series of short interactions.
The instrument is passed among the assistant surgeon, scrub nurse, and robot technician before being held by the scrub nurse.
The scrub nurse holds the surgical instrument for an extended period of over one minute.
The instrument is briefly exchanged between the scrub nurse and the circulator nurse.
The surgical instrument is passed between the circulator and scrub nurses before being held solely by the scrub nurse.
The robot technician and scrub nurse coordinate to handle and transfer the surgical instrument.
The assistant surgeon and scrub nurse repeatedly exchange the surgical instrument during a series of short interactions.
The robot technician and scrub nurse briefly share custody of the instrument before the scrub nurse holds it alone.
The circulator nurse, robot technician, and scrub nurse engage in a complex multi-party transfer of the surgical instrument.
The surgical instrument is passed between the robot technician, circulator nurse, and scrub nurse before being held by the scrub nurse for an extended period.
The assistant surgeon takes possession of the surgical instrument after a long idle period.

level2_segments.summary: The surgical instrument remains largely idle, held primarily by the scrub nurse with brief interactions involving the robot technician.
The instrument enters active use as it is rapidly exchanged among the head surgeon, assistant surgeon, and scrub nurse for surgical tasks.
Following a pause, the instrument undergoes a series of quick transfers between the surgical team before returning to an extended idle state with the scrub nurse.
The instrument is circulated among the nursing staff, robot technician, and assistant surgeon through a complex sequence of multi-party transfers and brief exchanges.
The assistant surgeon takes final possession of the instrument after a long period of inactivity.

After a long idle period, the technician briefly interacts with the MPS workstation.

The technician alternates between manipulating the robotic arm, infrared tracker, and workstation to finalize system adjustments.
The robot technician manipulates the MPS workstation after a brief initial pause.

The technician alternates between manipulating the robotic arm and holding a surgical instrument with short pauses, concluding with holding the instrument.

The technician actively manipulates the robotic arm and handles surgical instruments to support the ongoing procedure.

level1_segments.summary: The scrub nurse briefly holds a surgical instrument before a pause.
The scrub nurse intermittently prepares the instrument table and holds instruments with short pauses in between.
The scrub nurse actively manipulates the Mako robotic arm while holding various instruments, including a bone saw.
The scrub nurse alternates between holding instruments and preparing the table with short idle gaps.
The scrub nurse manipulates the Mako robotic arm and prepares instruments with brief pauses.
The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table and assists with patient preparation while holding surgical instruments.
The scrub nurse engages in intermittent preparation of the instrument table and holding instruments with short pauses in between.
The scrub nurse continuously prepares the instrument table, handles various surgical instruments and hammers, assists the surgeons, and calibrates the robotic arm with only brief pauses.
The nurse pauses briefly, assists the assistant surgeon, and then resumes preparing the instrument table.
The scrub nurse holds surgical instruments and prepares the table, with intermittent direct contact with the patient.
The nurse alternates between holding instruments and preparing the table with short pauses in between.
The scrub nurse briefly touches the patient and then adjusts the operating table.

level2_segments.summary: The scrub nurse performs initial instrument table preparation and holds instruments during the early stages of the procedure.
The nurse actively manipulates the Mako robotic arm and manages instruments during the bone work phase.
The scrub nurse focuses on extensive instrument table preparation and assists with patient positioning.
The nurse supports the surgical team by handling instruments, calibrating the robot, and adjusting the patient and operating table.

level1_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence including arm status, homing, accuracy, brake, and angle discrepancy checks.
The team performs surgery planning and bone registration, interspersed with multiple RIO checks and patient timeout periods.
Intraoperative planning is followed by a detailed bone preparation phase involving surface, anterior, posterior, and distal cuts with continuous RIO verification.
The system alternates between RIO checks and bone preparation activities for the distal, anterior, and posterior surfaces.
The robot monitor displays the intra-operative planning phase.
The system enters the start phase of the procedure.

level2_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence including arm status, homing, accuracy, brake, and angle discrepancy checks.
The team performs surgery planning and bone registration, interspersed with multiple RIO checks and patient timeout periods.
The procedure progresses through intraoperative planning, detailed bone preparation with continuous RIO verification, and the final start phase of the surgery.

robot setup (physical): Initial Calibration by MPS

active_predicates: Dressing the robot, to make it sterile
level0_segments.description: robot setup (physical): Dressing the robot, to make it sterile
robot setup (physical): Install base array by nurse
robot setup (physical): Install calibration array
robot setup (physical): Install actual Saw tip

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on to begin the setup process.
Initial calibration of the robot is performed by the MPS.
The robot is dressed for sterility, followed by the installation of the base array, calibration array, and the actual saw tip.

level2_segments.summary: The robot undergoes a complete setup process including power-on, initial calibration, and sterile dressing with instrument installation.

level0_segments.description: bone saw: scrub nurse is holding
bone saw: assistant surgeon is holding
bone saw: scrub nurse is holding
bone saw: head surgeon is holding
bone saw: head surgeon is holding; head surgeon is sawing

level1_segments.summary: The bone saw is held by the scrub nurse during an initial preparation phase.
The bone saw is held by the assistant surgeon after a long idle period.
The bone saw is returned to and held by the scrub nurse.
The head surgeon takes the bone saw and proceeds to use it for sawing bone.

level2_segments.summary: The bone saw undergoes a preparation and handoff phase where it is held by the scrub nurse and assistant surgeon before active use.
The head surgeon utilizes the bone saw to perform the primary bone cutting procedure.
